Title: Rice transcription factor bHLH25 confers resistance to multiple diseases by sensing H2O2

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubi promoter in protoplast transient expression
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Acts upstream in protoplasts to raise H2O2
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression and knockout in protoplast assays; H2O2 measurement; oxidation-specific immunoblot of bHLH25
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes H2O2 production that increases bHLH25 M256 oxidation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsRBOHA functions upstream of bHLH25 by producing H2O2. 2. Its overexpression increases, and its knockout decreases, bHLH25 M256 oxidation in protoplasts. 3. It was used to validate in vivo H2O2 sensing by bHLH25.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a primary nutrient-metabolism gene but a core upstream component experimentally used to validate the metabolically relevant H2O2-sensing mechanism

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsLAC7 is one of three H2O2-induced laccase genes required redundantly for lignin accumulation. 2. Overexpression increases lignin content, thicker sclerenchyma/cell walls, and blast resistance. 3. Triple knockout with OsLAC28/29 reduces lignin and weakens H2O2-induced disease resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in leaves after root H2O2 treatment and during pathogen infection
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf sclerenchyma cell walls / cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally redundant with OsLAC28 and OsLAC29; induced downstream of bHLH25-mediated miR397b repression
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidatively polymerizes monolignols to form lignin polymers in the cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes lignin accumulation and enhances disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: elevated lignin associated with thicker cell walls; excess lignin discussed as potentially growth inhibitory generally
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; overexpression; RT-qPCR; lignin measurement; histochemical staining; disease assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: exogenous H2O2 treatment; Magnaporthe oryzae infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be leveraged to enhance structural defense and blast resistance, likely best combined with regulatory modules controlling expression to avoid excess lignification

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsLAC28 is one of three H2O2-induced laccase genes redundantly promoting lignin biosynthesis. 2. Overexpression enhances lignin accumulation and disease resistance. 3. Contribution is revealed strongly in the OsLAC7/28/29 triple knockout background.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in leaves after H2O2 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf sclerenchyma cell walls / cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly with OsLAC7 and OsLAC29
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidatively polymerizes monolignols into lignin polymers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes lignin accumulation and disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; overexpression; RT-qPCR; lignin measurement; disease assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: exogenous H2O2 treatment; Magnaporthe oryzae infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for boosting lignified barrier defenses, preferably stacked with paralogs or their upstream regulator

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsLAC29 is one of three H2O2-induced laccases mediating lignin biosynthesis. 2. Overexpression increases lignin and resistance, whereas triple knockout with OsLAC7/28 lowers both. 3. Acts in the miR397b-laccase defense module downstream of bHLH25.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced in leaves after H2O2 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf sclerenchyma cell walls / cell wall
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally redundant with OsLAC7 and OsLAC28
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: oxidatively polymerizes monolignols into lignin polymers
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes lignin accumulation and disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; overexpression; RT-qPCR; lignin measurement; disease assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: exogenous H2O2 treatment; Magnaporthe oryzae infection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving barrier-mediated disease resistance in combination with OsLAC7/28 or upstream editing of miR397b/bHLH25

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Class II diterpene cyclase; copalyl diphosphate synthase
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPS2 is directly activated by bHLH25 via the pCPS2 N-box-like motif. 2. CPS2 overexpression increases phytocassane C and disease resistance, whereas cps2 knockout abolishes detectable phytocassane C and reduces resistance. 3. CPS2 mediates a phytoalexin branch independent of the miR397b-lignin pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated in bHLH25-OE plants; reduced in bhlh25-KO; induced mainly at 60–96 h post infection
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Changes in CPS2 alter phytoalexin but not lignin; contributes to bHLH25-dependent resistance but is not required for H2O2-induced resistance in the exogenous treatment assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: cyclizes geranylgeranyl diphosphate to copalyl diphosphate as a precursor step in phytocassane biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes phytocassane accumulation, cell death phenotype when overexpressed, and enhances disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for overexpression in rice; pCPS2 used in reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression associated with spontaneous cell death phenotype
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; overexpression; EMSA; transactivation assay; RT-qPCR; LC-ESI-MS/MS phytoalexin profiling; disease assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Magnaporthe oryzae infection; exogenous H2O2 treatment
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for enhancing antimicrobial phytoalexin production, but deployment should consider cell-death/growth tradeoffs and may benefit from dynamic or pathogen-inducible regulation

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: H2O2-sensing switch that coordinately controls lignin and phytoalexin accumulation through oxidation-state-dependent target selection
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression causes spontaneous cell death phenotype, likely via elevated phytoalexins; excess lignin/phytoalexin discussed as harmful when overaccumulated
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Functions in leaves during pathogen infection and H2O2 treatment; localized in nucleus
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional control of lignin biosynthesis and phytoalexin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: pathogen infection; exogenous H2O2; endogenous H2O2 via OsRBOHA; not responsive functionally under salt or high temperature stress
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: H2O2 produced upon pathogen attack; OsRBOHA-dependent ROS
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enhances disease resistance by repressing miR397b to derepress OsLAC7/28/29 and by activating CPS2
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Directly oxidized at methionine residues, especially M256, in the nucleus; oxidized form preferentially binds pmiR397b while non-oxidized form preferentially binds pCPS2; M256V abolishes DNA binding and function
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for broad-spectrum disease resistance engineering, preferably with precise tuning or inducible expression to avoid cell death and metabolite overaccumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bHLH25 directly binds the miR397b promoter to repress miR397b and increase OsLAC7/28/29-mediated lignin biosynthesis. 2. bHLH25 directly binds the CPS2 promoter to activate phytoalexin biosynthesis. 3. H2O2 directly oxidizes bHLH25 at M256, switching its DNA-binding preference and enabling sequential activation of two defense metabolite pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf cell walls for lignin; infected leaves for phytocassane
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: lignin reinforces cell walls against pathogen penetration; phytocassanes inhibit pathogen spore germination/hyphal growth
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter and native bHLH25 promoter used in rice; pmiR397b and pCPS2 used in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses miR397b; activates CPS2; indirectly increases OsLAC7/28/29 by repressing miR397b
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: transcription factor and direct H2O2 sensor
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional repression and activation controlled by methionine oxidation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Negative feedback/homeostatic loop: H2O2 oxidizes bHLH25 to promote lignin, lignin biosynthesis consumes H2O2, leading to non-oxidized bHLH25 accumulation and CPS2 activation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: yeast one-hybrid; DAP-qPCR; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; transactivation assay; CRISPR knockout; overexpression; complementation; oxidation-specific immunoblot; mass spectrometry; metabolite profiling; disease assay

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key repressor of the laccase module controlling lignin accumulation and thereby H2O2-induced disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression reduces lignin and disease resistance; knockout causes high resistance so exogenous H2O2 adds little further benefit
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Repressed by H2O2 and by bHLH25 in rice leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: represses lignin accumulation and weakens disease resistance by targeting OsLAC7/28/29
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its effects on disease resistance completely rely on suppression of OsLAC7/28/29; does not affect phytoalexin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Knockout or suppression could enhance lignin-mediated broad disease resistance, but lignification tradeoffs should be monitored
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miR397b directly targets OsLAC7/28/29 transcripts. 2. H2O2 and bHLH25 repress miR397b expression, thereby increasing laccase abundance and lignin. 3. miR397b knockout increases resistance while overexpression reduces resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf sclerenchyma cell walls / cell wall
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: reinforces cell wall to block pathogen penetration
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter for overexpression; pmiR397b used in reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of an H2O2-responsive regulatory module downstream of bHLH25; no direct autoregulation shown
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR knockout; overexpression; target-site reporter assay; RT-qPCR; genetic epistasis; lignin measurement; disease assay
